steps.text: Circle \(P\) has the same center \((3,6)\) and radius \(r_{P} = 2 \times r_{M} = 2 \times 2 = 4\), so \(r_{P}^{2} = 16\).

choices.explanation: • \((x-3)^{2}+(y-6)^{2}=8\) uses \(r^{2}=8\), giving \(r = 2\sqrt{2} \approx 2.83\), not twice circle \(M\)'s radius of 2. Not correct.
• \((x-3)^{2}+(y-6)^{2}=16\) has center \((3,6)\) (same as \(M\)) and \(r^{2}=16\) so \(r=4=2\times 2\). That matches choice B — B is correct.
• \((x-6)^{2}+(y-12)^{2}=4\) has center \((6,12)\), which differs from circle \(M\)'s center \((3,6)\). Not correct.
